package.name: motoman_hc20_support

description.p: ROS-Industrial support for the Motoman HC20 (and variants).
This package contains configuration data, 3D models and launch files for Motoman HC20DT, HC20DTP and HC30PL manipulators.

ul.li: HC20DTP - YR-1-06VXCP20-B00 (SPECIFICATION WITH BUILT-IN AIR HOSES, ETHERNET AND I/O CABLES)
HC30PL - YR-1-06VXCP30-B00 (BUILT-IN AIR HOSES, ETHERNET AND I/O CABLES SPECIFICATION)

description.p: Joint limits and maximum joint velocities are based on the information found in:

ul.li: MOTOMAN-HC20DT INSTRUCTIONS DUST-PROOF/DRIP-PROOF SPECIFICATION (HW1486129) version 190085-1CD, rev 4.
MOTOMAN-HC20DTP INSTRUCTIONS DUST-PROOF/DRIP-PROOF SPECIFICATION (HW2480394) version 193888-1CD, rev 6.
MOTOMAN-HC30PL INSTRUCTIONS DUST-PROOF/DRIP-PROOF SPECIFICATION (HW2480886) version 196230-1CD, rev 5.

description.p: All urdfs are based on the default motion and joint velocity limits, unless noted otherwise.

NOTE: the HC30PL is the palletizing variant of the HC20 where the wrist joints ('joint_4' and 'joint_5') are constrained to orient the flange normal to within +/-15 degrees with respect to the ground plane normal. Unfortunately, this limitation can't be modelled using URDF joint limits, and must therefore be handled by the motion planner or other process planning trajectories. Failure to take this constraint into account will cause the Yaskawa robot controller to abort trajectory execution and to raise alarms on the Teach Pendant. Additionally, the speed and joint limits of the robot explicitly vary depending on the payload. This URDF assumes no payload, i.e. max speed and joint limits. See the manual for more details.
Before using any of the configuration files and / or meshes included in this package, be sure to check they are correct for the particular robot model and configuration you intend to use them with.

package.author: Eric Marcil
package.maintainer: G.A. vd. Hoorn (TU Delft Robotics Institute)
